solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def evenOddBit(self, n: int) -> List[int]:
        even = 0
        odd = 0
        i = 0
        while n > 0:
            if (n & 1) == 1:
                if i % 2 == 0:
                    even += 1
                else:
                    odd += 1
            i += 1
            n >>= 1
        return [even, odd]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through the bits of the input integer `n` from right to left. For each bit set to 1, it increments the corresponding counter (`even` or `odd`) based on the bit's index. The loop continues until all bits are processed.  This approach leverages bitwise operations for efficiency, resulting in a time complexity of O(log n) due to the logarithmic nature of binary representation. Space complexity is O(1) as it uses a constant amount of extra space.
